sutta: [Music] the buddha said monks there are these 4 postures what for the posture of ghost pitas that of the luxurious that of the lion and the posture of the tata gata or buddha and of what sword monks is the posture of ghosts generally ghosts lie flat on their backs this is called the ghost posture and of what short monks is the posture of the luxurious generally the luxurious lie on the left side this is called the luxurious posture and of what's called monks is the posture of the lion the lion monks the king of beasts takes up a posture on his right side laying foot on foot and folding his tail between his thighs on waking he straightens out the 4 part of the body and looks round to the hinder part then monks if the lion king of beasts sees any part of his body displaced or disarranged thereupon monks the lion king of beasts is displeased but if the lion king of beasts sees no part of his body displaced or disarranged then he is pleased this monks is called the lion posture and of what sort monks is the tata gata posture in this case monks among aloof from sense desires aloo from unwholesome conditions enters on and abides in the first jhana and the second third and the fourth jana this is called the tata gata posture so there are these 4 postures that's the end of the suta
commentary: here the buddha is talking about posture being the sort of a position we take now when we take a wrestler and then the buddha said that ghosts lie on their backs so i'm sure many of us must have been born ghost before because we lie on our backs and the lying on the left side is the luxurious posture rely on the left side but that is not recommended by the buddha the buddha recommends that we take up the line posture like uh sleeping sideways on our right side and if you try it between the left and the right side you find that the right side generally is more comfortable maybe because when we rely on the left side now we put some pressure on our heart because our heart is slightly to the left of our body and the buddha sees that the buddha posture is instead of any particular body position the buddha always enters the jhanas the first second third and the fourth jhana and if you look through the sutas you investigate the sutas you find that the buddha's arahant disciples very often spend their days abiding in jhana they put an encourage them to spend their days abiding in jhana i never said that they are wasting their time never said that they were blessing out and you find the suta sometimes not like asari putta certain sutas as sometimes it is said that on certain days he abides in the first jhana certain days he abides in the second ghana days third and the fourth jannah etc and that is because the buddha encouraged them to constantly abide in jhana and attain very strong concentration because the buddha said the condition for wisdom is a mind that is bright and if we abide in jhana real ghana the mind is bright and when the mind is bright now all the hindus hindrances are abandoned so according to our vinaya the arahants are mindful 24 hours a day even when they go to sleep even when they take a rest they are mindful and this is hard for us to understand how a person can be asleep and still know that he is asleep and the way to that also of course is through the jaanas because the mind is so strong then it is mindful so we have to remember that the buddha always encourages us to abide in china then the nexus
